title: UCB — Financials
html:
UCB Financial Architecture

Governing Thought

UCB is a growth-phase account with expanding project volume across three TAs. ZoomRx revenue from UCB is not yet at enterprise scale but the trajectory is positive — each year since 2022 has added new workstreams. The client's own financial health is exceptional (€7.7B revenue, +26% YoY, EBITDA margin expanding to 34%), and R&D intensity (~30% of revenue) signals a company that invests heavily in evidence generation.

Where to Find Financial Data

System What It Holds Access Update Cadence
Kimble Revenue, project-level financials, effort tracking, invoicing for UCB projects Internal ZoomRx (web app) Real-time as PMs update
Cluster Management (OneDrive) Revenue/Wins/Pipeline by cluster & account; UCB's share of cluster revenue OneDrive, Base 1 file Weekly refresh

UCB Market Research Budget Intelligence

What We Know

Data Point Source Confidence
UCB total revenue: €7,741M (FY 2025) UCB FY 2025 press release High
R&D spend: ~30% of revenue (~€2.3B) UCB 2024 annual report Medium (2024 figure; 2025 may differ)
Adjusted EBITDA margin: 34% (FY 2025) UCB FY 2025 results High
Bimzelx alone: €2,220M+ (FY 2025) UCB FY 2025 results High

Interpretation

UCB's R&D intensity (~30% of revenue, ~€2.3B) is among the highest in the industry. Even if market research represents a fraction of R&D, the absolute budget potential is significant. For comparison:

  • J&J's estimated MR budget is $15M+ on ~$55B pharmaceutical revenue
  • UCB at €7.7B revenue with higher R&D intensity likely supports a meaningful MR budget

The company's growth trajectory (26% revenue growth, 79% EBITDA growth) suggests budget expansion, not compression. This is the opposite dynamic from cost-cutting accounts — UCB is in spend-to-grow mode.

Budget Philosophy Signals

  • "Utilize AI" mandate: UCB has enterprise AI initiatives (like most pharma), but the Patient Evidence leadership rotation (CCO → Patient Evidence) suggests investment in evidence-based commercial capabilities
  • Multi-indication launch pace: 5 Bimzelx approvals in 13 months means multiple concurrent launch support needs — each requiring market research
  • Neurona acquisition ($1.15B): Willingness to make large bets signals a company comfortable with significant investment

Pricing Architecture

No preferred vendor discount or MSA arrangement is documented for UCB. Projects appear to be priced on a per-engagement basis.

Implication: There is an opportunity to propose an MSA or preferred vendor arrangement as the project count grows. The Bimzelx Derm PET + Rheum PET + potential HS PET creates natural bundling leverage.

Revenue Context

ZoomRx revenue from UCB is distributed across 5 active and several completed projects spanning 3 TAs. Specific revenue figures should be verified against Kimble. The account is growing in terms of concurrent active projects:

  • 2022-2023: 2-3 concurrent projects (Briviact focused)
  • 2024: 3-4 concurrent projects (added Bimzelx HCP-Pt, gMG HCP-Pt)
  • 2025: 3-4 concurrent projects (Fintepla PET, Nayzilam ATU)
  • 2026 YTD: 2-3 active projects (Bimzelx Derm PET, Bimzelx Rheum PET) with pipeline potential

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
